ogTitle: Garners Beach vs Garrawalt: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Garners Beach and Garrawalt.
heading: Garners Beach vs Garrawalt — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Garners Beach, Queensland and Garrawalt, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Garners Beach has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Garrawalt (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Garners Beach or Garrawalt safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Garners Beach has a flood risk score of 58/100, while Garrawalt scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Garners Beach scores 28/100 for bushfire risk. Garrawalt scores 61/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Garners Beach or Garrawalt?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Garners Beach has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
